item1.parentNote: No predecessors and no parent entities

affiliates.name: KDKC, Inc.
affiliates.products: packaging supplies, shipping boxes, custom software

item1.businessDescription: Customer-appreciation/follow-up cookie gifting program. Franchisees sell to business customers, prepare/bake/package cookies for mailing via USPS with customized messages/logos. Operates from approved retail location (Bakery) in specified geographic area.

provisions: refusal to renew requires compensation in some cases
refusal to permit transfer except for good cause

item5.initialPackagingPurchaseNote: Purchased from affiliate KDKC, uniform, non-refundable
item5.refundNote: Fully earned and non-refundable under any circumstances
item5.stateDeferralNote: Some states impose fee deferral — see Exhibit G

royaltyMinimum.formula: Greater of 6% of Gross Revenue or $800/month beginning month 13

item6.phaseChangeNote: First 12 months = 6% only. Month 13 onward = max(6%, $800/month minimum)

includes: full redemption value of gift certificates/coupons even if third-party sellers retain fees

item6.technologyFee: $100/month (due one month in advance with royalty)
item6.technologyFeeCovers: custom software, website hosting, other services
item6.technologyFeeEscalation: Can increase for updated software, added technology, upgrades, third-party vendor increases

otherFees.name: Audit cost
otherFees.amount: cost plus understatement plus legal/accounting plus 1.5%/month late fee

otherFees.name: Conference fee
otherFees.amount: then-current rate, even if not attending

lineItems.note: High recommended if opening in Q4 for Christmas

affiliateSupplierDetails.name: KDKC, Inc.
affiliateSupplierDetails.products: Packaging supplies, shipping boxes, Cookie Advantage Business Software

item8.purchaseCooperativeNote: None as of issuance, but franchisor may negotiate alternative arrangements

rebatesFromSuppliers.note: No cap on maximum rebates franchisor may receive from suppliers based on franchisee purchases

preOpeningSupport: Required operating assets and suppliers identification

item12.territoryNote: Franchise tied to specified geographic area/territory. Franchisor designates territory before opening. Full Item 12 protection/competition language not surfaced — do not force boolean from partial text.
item12.unresolvedReason: Item 12 territorial protection and competition language not fully surfaced in retrieved text

nonCurableDefaults: 3+ NSF/returned checks in one calendar year
Failure to submit reports on 3 occasions in 12 months
postTerminationObligations: Pay outstanding amounts including balance of royalties from termination through scheduled expiration
Hand over customer files including sensitive customer/payment information
item17.futureRoyaltiesNote: On termination, franchisee must pay balance of royalties from termination through scheduled expiration — major burden

H.name: Contracts for use with the franchise

provisions: Refusal to renew requires compensation in some cases
Refusal to permit transfer except for good cause

universalRules: Royalty must support phase-change formula: months 1-12 = flat %, month 13+ = max(%, minimum floor)
Initial investment should separate franchise fee from affiliate-paid initial purchases (packaging)
Fee model must support CPI adjustment with threshold, max adjustments/year, and notice period
Gross revenue definition matters: business interruption insurance proceeds and full gift certificate value are unusual inclusions
Affiliate-as-sole-supplier with clean revenue disclosure ($177,913.82) is a teaching example for supplier economics
Item 8 percent-of-purchases under specs (40-50% opening, 80-90% operating) is a structured teaching pair
Future royalties through scheduled expiration after termination is a major burden — do not miss
Territory should remain unresolved when Item 12 text not fully surfaced — never force boolean from partial read
Unresolved items with confidence=low are first-class records, not gaps to hide
Item 9 obligations matrix is a routing map for clause harvesting — consume it
